name: Tetragon (Cilium)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Isovalent (Cisco) offers commercial support

auth.notes: gRPC API on localhost; mTLS for remote access; Kubernetes RBAC for pod security policies

performance.notes: eBPF-based: negligible overhead on kernel events; near-zero performance impact on workloads

agent_readiness.notes: eBPF-based security observability and runtime enforcement for Kubernetes. Kernel-level visibility: process execution, file access, network connections, privilege escalation. TracingPolicies for custom rules. gRPC + JSON event streaming. Low overhead. Part of Cilium ecosystem. Runtime security + observability in one tool.

gotchas: Kubernetes-only deployment; Linux kernel 4.19+ required for full features
eBPF expertise required to write custom TracingPolicies
gRPC API less accessible than REST APIs for non-Go teams
Large volume of events requires careful filtering and storage planning
best_when: Kubernetes runtime security monitoring at kernel level
Zero-trust security posture enforcement for containers
Security incident investigation with kernel-level process/network visibility
Compliance scenarios requiring immutable audit trail of container behavior
avoid_when: Teams without Linux/eBPF expertise for policy writing
Simple container security scanning (use Trivy/Grype instead)
summary: Tetragon is Cilium's eBPF-based runtime security and observability tool for Kubernetes. Provides kernel-level visibility into process execution, file access, network connections, and privilege escalation with near-zero overhead. TracingPolicies for custom enforcement rules. gRPC/JSON event streaming. Part of the Cilium ecosystem. Best for Kubernetes security teams wanting immutable kernel-level audit trails and runtime enforcement.
